description:
You are acting as a log-analyst who has been asked to perform a very quick diagnostic check from the command line.

    1. Locate the log file stored at  
       /home/user/logs/webapp.log

    2. Count how many times the exact, case-sensitive string  
       PAYMENT_FAILURE  
       occurs anywhere in that file.

    3. Write the result to the new file  
       /home/user/diagnostics/payment_failure.count

       Output format requirements (must match **exactly**, including the final newline):
       • The file must contain just one line.  
       • The line must be of the form

         PAYMENT_FAILURE_COUNT=<NUMBER>

       where <NUMBER> is the decimal count you obtained.  
       • No leading spaces, no trailing spaces, no extra text, and the file must end with a single Unix newline.

    4. Do not output anything else to the terminal except what is naturally printed by the command(s) you decide to use.

    A single, well-chosen command is enough to finish this task.

        (newline shown as \n)
    </expected>
